description_long: This house features a symmetrical brick facade with white trim and a central recessed section, flanked by two projecting wings. The roof is a dark gray gable style, and the building has two main stories with regularly spaced rectangular windows. The front elevation includes arched windows and a small set of steps leading to the main entryway, which is slightly set back. On the rear side, a large cylindrical structure, likely a stairwell or utility shaft, is attached, adding a modern touch to the otherwise traditional design. The side elevations are simpler, with fewer windows and a clear view of the brickwork and roof overhangs. The overall appearance blends classic townhouse elements with some contemporary features.
description: A two-story brick house with a gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a cylindrical rear extension, blending traditional and modern design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical brick facade, arched windows, and central recessed entryway with steps.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the gable end, simple brickwork, and minimal window openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the cylindrical extension, regular window arrangement, and a secondary entry or utility area.
description_view_3: Right side view with the cylindrical structure attached, gable roof, and a mix of window sizes.
attribute_entryway_design: Central recessed entry with steps, arched windows, and minimal decorative elements
